# OpenRates Agent Gateway — Full Technical Reference

OpenRates gives AI agents and applications trustworthy current and historical currency
exchange rates. Every result includes its source, effective date, freshness classification,
rate type, confidence score, and rounding details. Official daily reference rates are
explicitly never labeled as live market rates.

## Architecture

Monorepo packages:

- @openrates/schemas — Zod schemas, error model, API envelopes, config validation
- @openrates/observability — Pino logging with secret redaction
- @openrates/currency-metadata — Currency catalogue, aliases, and search
- @openrates/core — Money arithmetic, rate math, freshness, confidence, fees
- @openrates/provider-interface — Provider contract and reusable contract test suite
- @openrates/provider-frankfurter — Frankfurter adapter for ECB official rates
- @openrates/cache — In-memory LRU and pluggable Redis store
- @openrates/router — Provider registry, selection, fallback, and rate engine
- @openrates/api — Fastify REST API with OpenAPI 3.1 and interactive docs
- @openrates/mcp — MCP server with six tools, nine resources, two transports
- @openrates/sdk — TypeScript client with retries and AbortSignal support
- @openrates/cli — CLI with rate, convert, series, currencies, doctor commands

## MCP server

Primary interface for agents. Supports stdio (local) and Streamable HTTP (remote).

### Tools

**convert_currency**
Input: { amount: string, from: string, to: string, date?: string,
  mode?: "official"|"market"|"auto", useCase?: string,
  datePolicy?: "previous_available"|"strict", allowFallback?: boolean,
  maxAgeSeconds?: number, fixedFee?: string, percentageFee?: string,
  spreadPercentage?: string, responseDetail?: "compact"|"standard"|"full" }
Output: conversion result with rate, convertedAmount, rateType, effectiveDate,
  provider, freshnessClass, isLive, confidenceScore, warnings, disclaimer

**get_exchange_rate**
Input: { base: string, quote: string, date?: string, mode?: string,
  provider?: string, useCase?: string, datePolicy?: string,
  allowFallback?: boolean, maxAgeSeconds?: number,
  responseDetail?: "compact"|"standard"|"full" }
Output: normalized rate with all metadata

**get_exchange_rate_series**
Input: { base: string, quote: string, startDate: string, endDate: string,
  interval?: "day"|"week"|"month", mode?: string, provider?: string,
  fillPolicy?: "none"|"previous", responseDetail?: string }
Output: series with points array; each point has date, rate, filled flag

**list_currencies**
Input: { query?: string, status?: "active"|"retired"|"all",
  provider?: string, limit?: number }
Output: array of currencies with code, name, minorUnits, status, aliases

**compare_exchange_rate_providers**
Input: { base: string, quote: string, date?: string,
  providers?: string[], mode?: string, maxProviders?: number }
Output: per-provider results with rate, confidence, freshness; median calculation;
  disagreement flag if rates diverge beyond OPENRATES_PROVIDER_DISAGREEMENT_PERCENT

**explain_exchange_rate**
Input: { topic: "official_vs_market"|"buy_vs_sell"|"mid_market"|"fees_and_spread"|
  "freshness"|"cross_rate"|"weekend_rate"|"currency_alias"|"provider_difference",
  context?: { base?: string, quote?: string, provider?: string } }
Output: plain-language explanation suitable for including in a user-facing answer

### Resources (read-only)

openrates://docs/quickstart — Getting started
openrates://docs/tool-selection — Which tool to use for which task
openrates://docs/rate-types — official_reference vs market rates explained
openrates://docs/freshness — Freshness classes and thresholds
openrates://docs/errors — Error codes and recovery suggestions
openrates://providers — Configured providers and their capabilities
openrates://currencies — All supported currencies with metadata
openrates://capabilities — Server and provider capability summary
openrates://license — License and attribution

### Server instructions (summary)

Use OpenRates whenever a task requires a current or historical exchange rate.
Do not use model memory for rates. Use official mode for accounting, invoices, reporting.
Always mention the effective date and rate type. Never describe an official daily rate as
live. Ask for clarification only when the currency itself is ambiguous.

## REST API

All endpoints under /v1. Default port 3000.

### Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | /v1/rates | Exchange rate for a pair |
| GET | /v1/rates/series | Historical time series |
| POST | /v1/convert | Convert an amount |
| GET | /v1/currencies | List or search currencies |
| GET | /v1/currencies/:code | One currency |
| GET | /v1/providers | Configured providers |
| GET | /v1/providers/:providerId | One provider |
| GET | /v1/capabilities | Server and provider capabilities |
| GET | /v1/health | Liveness probe |
| GET | /v1/ready | Readiness probe |
| GET | /openapi.json | OpenAPI 3.1 document |
| GET | /docs | Interactive API reference |

### Example requests

```bash
# Get a rate
curl "http://localhost:3000/v1/rates?base=USD&quote=EUR&date=latest"

# Convert an amount
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/convert \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount":"1000.00","from":"USD","to":"EUR","mode":"official"}'

# Historical series
curl "http://localhost:3000/v1/rates/series?base=USD&quote=EUR&startDate=2026-01-01&endDate=2026-03-31"

# Search currencies
curl "http://localhost:3000/v1/currencies?query=riyal"
```

### Response envelope

Success:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { ... },
  "requestId": "req_...",
  "generatedAt": "2026-06-15T20:00:00.000Z"
}
```

Failure:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "UNSUPPORTED_CURRENCY",
    "message": "The currency \"XYZ\" is not supported.",
    "retryable": false,
    "suggestion": "Use a valid ISO 4217 currency code."
  },
  "requestId": "req_..."
}
```

### Response detail levels

compact — Rate, effectiveDate, provider, main warning. Minimal payload.
standard (default) — Adds confidence, freshnessClass, calculationMethod, all warnings.
full — Adds all timestamps, cache metadata, route decision, inverse rate, confidence factors.

### Error codes

| Code | Retryable | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| INVALID_REQUEST | false | Malformed request body or query |
| INVALID_AMOUNT | false | Amount is not a valid decimal string |
| INVALID_DATE | false | Date is not ISO 8601 or "latest" |
| AMBIGUOUS_CURRENCY | false | Name matches multiple currencies; details.candidates lists them |
| UNSUPPORTED_CURRENCY | false | Currency not found in catalogue |
| UNSUPPORTED_PAIR | false | Provider does not support this pair |
| UNSUPPORTED_MODE | false | Provider does not support the requested mode |
| UNSUPPORTED_DATE_RANGE | false | Range exceeds the configured maximum |
| PROVIDER_NOT_CONFIGURED | false | Requested provider is not registered |
| PROVIDER_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED | false | Provider rejected the API key |
| PROVIDER_RATE_LIMITED | true | Provider returned 429 |
| PROVIDER_TIMEOUT | true | Provider did not respond in time |
| PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE | true | Provider returned 5xx or is unreachable |
| RATE_NOT_AVAILABLE | false | No provider has data for this pair |
| RATE_TOO_STALE | false | Rate is older than maxAgeSeconds |
| STRICT_DATE_NOT_AVAILABLE | false | No rate published for exact date; use previous_available |
| FALLBACK_NOT_ALLOWED | false | Primary failed but allowFallback is false |
| CACHE_ERROR | true | Cache read or write failed |
| INTERNAL_ERROR | false | Unexpected server error |

## Freshness classification

### Time fields

effectiveDate — The date the rate applies to. Always present.
observedAt — When a market value was observed. Only from providers that supply it.
publishedAt — When the provider published the value. Only from providers that supply it.
retrievedAt — When OpenRates fetched it. Always recorded.

### Freshness classes

live — A market rate observed inside the live threshold.
recent — A market rate observed inside the recent threshold.
latest_available — The most current value the provider has, from today.
latest_business_day — The latest value from an earlier business day (weekend or holiday).
historical_exact_date — The published value for the exact requested date.
historical_previous_available_date — The previous published value when the exact date had none.
stale — Older than the allowed age. Must not be treated as current.
unknown — No timestamp from the provider; liveness cannot be proven.

### isLive: true conditions (all must hold)

1. Provider explicitly supports intraday rates (capabilities.supportsIntraday = true).
2. Provider supplies a reliable observedAt or publishedAt timestamp.
3. The rate's age is within OPENRATES_LIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS (default 300).
4. The rate type is market data, not official_reference.

Official daily reference rates never satisfy these conditions.

### Default thresholds

OPENRATES_LIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS=300
OPENRATES_RECENT_THRESHOLD_SECONDS=3600

## Confidence score

Range: 0.0 to 1.0.
Labels: high (>=0.85), medium (>=0.6), low (<0.6).

Weights:
- Provider trust: 40%
- Freshness match for use case: 20%
- Source timestamp present: 15%
- Provider health: 10%
- Direct pair (not cross or inverse): 10%
- Cross-provider agreement: 5%

Stale rates are capped at 0.59 (below medium threshold).
Computed cross-rates reduce confidence.
Provider disagreement above OPENRATES_PROVIDER_DISAGREEMENT_PERCENT triggers a warning and
reduces confidence.

## Decimal arithmetic

All monetary calculations use Decimal.js at 50-digit precision. Native JavaScript
floating-point is never used in monetary paths.

Rounding mode: half-even (banker's rounding).
Rounding target: target currency minor units (e.g., 2 for USD, 0 for JPY).
If minor units are unknown, 2 is used with a warning.

Fee order: gross amount → spread percentage → percentage fee → fixed fee → round.

Rates and amounts are strings in JSON responses. Never parse them as JavaScript numbers.

## Provider contract

Every adapter implements ExchangeRateProvider:

capabilities() → ProviderCapabilities
  supportedModes: ("official"|"market")[]
  supportedRateTypes: RateType[]
  supportsHistorical: boolean
  supportsTimeSeries: boolean
  supportsIntraday: boolean
  supportsProviderTimestamp: boolean
  requiresApiKey: boolean
  estimatedUpdateFrequencySeconds?: number
  supportedCurrencies?: string[]

healthCheck() → ProviderHealth
  status: "healthy"|"degraded"|"unavailable"
  checkedAt: string (ISO 8601)
  latencyMs?: number
  lastSuccessfulRequestAt?: string
  message?: string

getLatestRate(input) → ProviderRate
  baseCurrency, quoteCurrency, rate (decimal string), rateType,
  effectiveDate, calculationMethod, providerId,
  observedAt?, publishedAt?, sourceIds?, metadata?

getHistoricalRate(input) → ProviderRate
  Same as getLatestRate plus datePolicy handling.
  If datePolicy is "strict" and date has no rate: throw STRICT_DATE_NOT_AVAILABLE.
  If datePolicy is "previous_available": return prior date and report actual effectiveDate.

getTimeSeries(input) → ProviderTimeSeries
  baseCurrency, quoteCurrency, startDate, endDate, rateType, providerId,
  points: { date, rate, filled }[]

listCurrencies() → ProviderCurrency[]
  code, name

normalizeError(error) → OpenRatesError
  Must map timeout → PROVIDER_TIMEOUT
  429 → PROVIDER_RATE_LIMITED
  5xx → PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE
  auth failure → PROVIDER_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED
  unsupported pair → UNSUPPORTED_PAIR

Adapter requirements: request timeouts, retry only safe GETs with bounded exponential
backoff, never log API keys, include provider attribution on every response, pass the
reusable contract test suite from @openrates/provider-interface/testing with fixtures.

## Frankfurter provider (default)

Publishes ECB official reference rates. Free, no API key required.

supportedModes: ["official"]
supportedRateTypes: ["official_reference"]
supportsHistorical: true
supportsTimeSeries: true
supportsIntraday: false
supportsProviderTimestamp: false
estimatedUpdateFrequencySeconds: 86400

Weekend/holiday behavior:
- datePolicy "previous_available" (default): returns prior business day, reports actual date
- datePolicy "strict": returns STRICT_DATE_NOT_AVAILABLE with suggestion

Configuration:
OPENRATES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER=frankfurter
FRANKFURTER_BASE_URL=https://api.frankfurter.dev
FRANKFURTER_TIMEOUT_MS=5000

Known limitations: no intraday, no bid/ask, rates are midpoints, limited currency
coverage, no provider timestamp.

## Cache layer

Default: in-memory LRU, 10,000 items.
Optional: Redis via REDIS_URL.

TTLs:
- Latest official rate: 15 minutes (900 s)
- Historical rate: 7 days (604800 s)
- Historical series: 1 day (86400 s)
- Provider capabilities: 1 hour (3600 s)
- Provider health: 30 seconds
- Currency metadata: 24 hours (86400 s)

Cache keys include: provider, mode, base, quote, date.
Cache response metadata: hit (bool), storedAt (ISO 8601), ageSeconds.

## Currency metadata

Catalogue covers ISO 4217 active and retired currencies.

Each entry: code, numericCode?, name, minorUnits, status, countries, symbols, aliases,
introducedAt?, retiredAt?, replacedBy?.

Features:
- Lookup by code (exact)
- Lookup by name (case-insensitive)
- Lookup by alias (e.g., "buck" → USD, "euro" → EUR)
- Lookup by country name (e.g., "Japan" → JPY)
- Ambiguity detection ("dollar" → [USD, CAD, AUD, ...])
- Retired currency guidance (TRL → TRY with retiredAt and replacedBy)
- CNY / CNH distinction for onshore/offshore

## Configuration reference

NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3000
HOST=0.0.0.0

OPENRATES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER=frankfurter
OPENRATES_PROVIDER_ORDER=frankfurter
OPENRATES_ALLOW_FALLBACK=true

FRANKFURTER_BASE_URL=https://api.frankfurter.dev
FRANKFURTER_TIMEOUT_MS=5000

OPENRATES_CACHE_DRIVER=memory
OPENRATES_MEMORY_CACHE_MAX_ITEMS=10000
REDIS_URL=

OPENRATES_LIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS=300
OPENRATES_RECENT_THRESHOLD_SECONDS=3600
OPENRATES_PROVIDER_DISAGREEMENT_PERCENT=1.0

OPENRATES_LOG_LEVEL=info
OPENRATES_LOG_AMOUNTS=false
OPENRATES_TELEMETRY=false

OPENRATES_API_AUTH_ENABLED=false
OPENRATES_API_KEYS=

OPENRATES_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false
OPENRATES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE=120

OPENRATES_MAX_SERIES_POINTS=2000
OPENRATES_MAX_DAILY_RANGE_YEARS=5

Market provider convention:
OPENRATES_PROVIDER_<NAME>_API_KEY=
OPENRATES_PROVIDER_<NAME>_BASE_URL=
OPENRATES_PROVIDER_<NAME>_ENABLED=false
